title: Missing column name in select expands to all columns in table
description: When executing a select query that involves a missing column (i.e. a column name that does not exist in the table), the missing column is expanded into all columns in the entire table under some circumstances.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), age INT, salary DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test (id, name, age, salary) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30, 50000.00);
INSERT INTO test (id, name, age, salary) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 25, 60000.50);
SELECT id, NULL AS non_existent_column FROM test ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, name, (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM test) AS avg_salary FROM test WHERE age > 20;
key_bug_features: Missing column in select expands to all table columns
